rocolegal.challenge: The law firm had an outdated website that didn't convey professionalism. The structure was confusing and contact forms weren't prominent.
rocolegal.solution: Redesigned the digital presence with a bilingual WordPress site, clear service pages, and calls to action throughout the user journey.
rocolegal.result: A professional site that better reflects the firm's expertise, improves search visibility, and converts more visits into qualified enquiries.

vilaLancisV2.challenge: The advisory firm had a slow, outdated website that didn't properly explain their services. Poor UX was hurting SEO and client acquisition.
vilaLancisV2.solution: Complete redesign in WordPress, reorganizing content by service type, adding multilingual support, and working on technical SEO.
vilaLancisV2.result: A clear, fast website presenting all services in an organized way, making it easy for businesses and freelancers to get in touch.

decos.challenge: The online store was limited by an outdated platform. Managing products required too much technical effort and was hurting sales.
decos.solution: Migration from Prestashop to WordPress with WooCommerce, optimizing performance, navigation, and product pages.
decos.result: A more agile, easier-to-manage store with better shopping experience and a solid technical base to keep growing.

fenix.challenge: The mentoring programme needed a website that conveyed authority, explained the value proposition, and captured leads organically.
fenix.solution: WordPress site with conversion-oriented structure, SEO work, and Systeme.io integration for lead capture.
fenix.result: A website that clearly communicates the offer, improves search visibility, and channels visitors into the programme.

vibrantskin.challenge: The brand needed a polished e-commerce experience for microbiome-friendly skincare and supplements, with clear product storytelling and performance that supports conversions and international orders.
vibrantskin.solution: Built a clean Shopify store with custom Liquid sections, structured collections and navigation, mobile-first UX, and technical SEO plus optimized imagery.
vibrantskin.result: A fast, conversion-focused store that presents the line clearly, reinforces the science-led positioning, and supports shipping and merchandising without friction.

ariadnaVilalta.challenge: An international cyberpsychology expert needed a credible site that explains a complex field—neurotechnology, ethics, and mental health—and supports speaking, media, and a book launch.
ariadnaVilalta.solution: WordPress site with a clear narrative: pillars, talk topics, Q&A, and press appearances, structured for SEO and easy scanning on every device.
ariadnaVilalta.result: Strong expert positioning online: institutions, media, and readers can quickly understand her focus on neurorights and digital mental health, and find ways to collaborate.

about.title: About me
about.description: I'm Florencia Rímolo, a computer engineer from UPC Barcelona with over 10 years of experience in web development. I've worked in fintech, university systems, and delivered 30+ freelance projects. Today, under the brand RimoByte, I help SMBs and freelancers across Spain build websites that work: fast, professional, and easy to maintain.

highlights.enterprise: Experience in fintech and university systems

howMuch.question: How much does a website cost?
howMuch.answer: It depends on the type of project. A corporate website starts from €800, an online store from €1,200, and a custom web app from €2,500. I provide a detailed quote before starting, with no surprises.

howLong.question: How long does a web project take?
howLong.answer: A landing page can be ready in 1-2 weeks. A corporate website in 3-4 weeks. An online store in 4-6 weeks. Exact timelines depend on scope, but we always agree on them before starting.

onlyWordPress.question: Do you only work with WordPress?
onlyWordPress.answer: No. WordPress is one of my specialties for corporate websites and online stores, but I also work with Vue, Nuxt, Astro, and Shopify for projects that need a different approach.

canMaintain.question: Can I maintain the website myself?
canMaintain.answer: Yes. All websites are designed so you can easily manage content. I also offer monthly maintenance if you'd rather not handle the technical side.

remote.question: Do you work remotely?
remote.answer: Yes. I work remotely with clients across Spain. Communication is direct via email, WhatsApp, or video call — whatever you prefer.

whatMaintenance.question: What does maintenance include?
whatMaintenance.answer: WordPress and plugin updates, backups, security monitoring, and technical support to resolve any issues. From €40/month.

templates.title: Templates & Resources
templates.intro: Professional templates used in real projects. Ready-to-use development tools built with best practices and modern technologies.

contact.title: Tell me about your project
contact.subtitle: Let's work together
contact.description: No commitment. I reply within 24h by email or instantly via WhatsApp.

footer.tagline: Professional web development for SMBs and freelancers in Spain.

rocolegal.description: A law firm needed a modern digital presence to reflect professionalism and attract clients. Launched an elegant, bilingual site that builds trust and generates inquiries.

upnext.title: UpNext - Discover movies and series on streaming
upnext.shortDescription: Discover movies and series available on streaming platforms in Spain with personalized recommendations.
upnext.description: UpNext is a web application to discover movies and series and find out which streaming platforms they are available on in Spain. It allows you to save preferences, discard content and receive personalized recommendations to decide what to watch without wasting time.

jlgki.title: JLG K&I - Help organizations transform their knowledge assets into competitive advantages
jlgki.shortDescription: WordPress site optimized for SEO and speed helping organizations transform knowledge assets.
jlgki.description: Design and development of a WordPress site for JLG K&I, a company that helps organizations transform their knowledge assets into competitive advantages. The site is built with WordPress and is optimized for SEO and speed.

vibrantskin.title: Vibrant Skin - Online Store for Natural Skincare Products
vibrantskin.shortDescription: Clean conversion-oriented Shopify store for natural skincare with custom Liquid sections and mobile-first UX.
vibrantskin.description: End-to-end design and development of a clean, conversion-oriented Shopify store for natural skincare. Custom Liquid sections, product taxonomy and filters, optimized collections, and mobile-first UX. Configured payments, shipping rules, and email flows. SEO fundamentals (schema, meta, alt), image compression and lazy loading to improve speed.

bandidamezcal.title: Bandida - Online Store for Mezcal Drinks
bandidamezcal.shortDescription: Bold Shopify store for mezcal with custom product pages, localization for MX/US, and payment integrations.
bandidamezcal.description: Design and development of a bold Shopify store for mezcal beverages. Custom product pages with tasting notes, bundle options and age-gate. Localization, tax and shipping setup for MX/US, integration with Mercado Pago/Stripe, and automated emails. Technical SEO and performance optimization across Liquid templates and assets.

reset7.title: RESET7 - Personal Trainer Landing
reset7.shortDescription: Landing page with smooth animations, advanced SEO strategy, and automated marketing campaigns.
reset7.description: Complete design and development of the landing page for personal trainer Juancar Garma. Designed from scratch with Elementor, smooth animations and micro-interactions. Implementation of advanced SEO strategy with Yoast, speed optimization and automated marketing campaign with MailerLite.

decos.title: de Cos - Online Store for Handcrafted Jute Sandals
decos.shortDescription: Migration from Prestashop to WordPress with WooCommerce, improving performance, customization and security.
decos.description: Migration of online store for handcrafted jute sandals from Prestashop to WordPress with WooCommerce, achieving improvements in performance, customization and security. Implementation of newsletter for customers with news and seasonal discounts.

rockzone.title: Rockzone Camp - Rock Camp Website
rockzone.shortDescription: Rock camp website with intelligent forms and impactful design for children and teenagers.
rockzone.description: Website for rock camp in Cádiz designed and developed from Hostinger Builder to WordPress, adding functionalities like forms with intelligent logic. Impactful and attractive design thought for children and teenagers.

luciaNails.title: Lucía Nails - Nail and Aesthetics Center
luciaNails.shortDescription: Customized WordPress website reflecting unique brand identity with performance and SEO optimization.
luciaNails.description: Website for nail and aesthetics center designed and developed from WordPress template, completely customized to reflect the unique brand identity. Performance and SEO optimization to ensure speed and good positioning.

vilaLancisV2.title: Vila & Lancis - Tax, Labor and Accounting Advisory
vilaLancisV2.shortDescription: Complete WordPress redesign with multilingual support, PageSpeed optimization, and advanced SEO work.
vilaLancisV2.description: Complete redesign of Vila & Lancis website from scratch with WordPress, improving speed and user experience. Added multilingual support, PageSpeed optimization, SEO work with keyword review and image compression for optimal performance.

vilaLancisV1.title: Vila i Lancis (v1) - Provisional Version
vilaLancisV1.shortDescription: Provisional Astro version with Resend email integration developed while building WordPress version.
vilaLancisV1.description: Initial version of Vila i Lancis developed with Astro and Resend for emails. Provisional solution while developing the WordPress version, copying existing design and texts due to problems with translations and admin panel access of the previous WordPress.

fenix.title: Fénix – Motherhood and productive life
fenix.shortDescription: WordPress site for a mentoring programme integrating motherhood and entrepreneurship, with SEO and lead capture via Systeme.io.
fenix.description: Website for Fénix, a mentoring programme for entrepreneurial and executive mothers who want to integrate motherhood and productive life. Built with WordPress and Elementor, optimized for SEO to drive organic sales and connected to Systeme.io for lead capture and management. Clear structure by programme levels (Despertar, Construcción, Transformación) and conversion-focused CTAs.

ariadnaVilalta.title: Ariadna Vilalta - Cyberpsychology, speaking, and authorship
ariadnaVilalta.shortDescription: Professional site for an international cyberpsychology expert: conferences, research themes, book promotion, and media presence.
ariadnaVilalta.description: Website for Ariadna Vilalta, cyberpsychologist, speaker, and author, focused on how neurotechnology and artificial intelligence intersect with mental health, ethics, and human rights. Built with WordPress and Elementor, with a structure for pillars, talk topics, Q&A, and press coverage, plus SEO fundamentals so institutions, organizers, and readers can discover her work and book.

freelance.title: Freelance Web Developer
freelance.description: Development of products and websites using the most appropriate technology for each project. Work with WordPress and Shopify for content and e-commerce solutions, and with modern frontend (Vue, Nuxt, Astro) for custom interfaces and web products. Experience in end-to-end projects: architecture, development, optimization and deployment.

ithinkupc.title: Software Engineer | iThinkUPC
ithinkupc.description: Development of systems for the Polytechnic University of Catalonia. Responsibilities included project management, development of university electoral systems and digital identity platforms. Team work with agile methodologies.

flanks.title: Software Engineer | Flanks
flanks.description: Backend development and scraping in the fintech sector. Bank aggregator integration, API development and frontend work. Client meetings and cloud deployment.

inlabfib.description: Development and maintenance of REST APIs. Maintenance of internal university systems. Final degree project: web application for managing academic stays, currently in production.

projects.title: Some completed projects
projects.context: A selection of real projects developed in WordPress.

rocolegal.problem: The law firm had an outdated website that didn’t convey the level of trust or professionalism of their legal work. The structure was unclear and contact forms weren’t prominent, making it difficult for potential clients to find the right service and request a consultation.
rocolegal.proposal: Redesign the firm’s online presence with a bilingual WordPress site, clear service pages by practice area, team profiles and visible calls to action across the site so visitors can get in touch from different points of the journey.
rocolegal.result: An elegant, professional website that better reflects the firm’s expertise, improves visibility in search engines and turns more visits into qualified enquiries via forms and direct contact.

vilaLancisV2.problem: The accounting and tax advisory firm had a slow, outdated website that didn’t properly explain all their tax, labour and accounting services. The user experience was confusing and poor performance hurt both SEO and the trust of potential clients.
vilaLancisV2.proposal: Redesign the website from scratch in WordPress, reorganising content by service type, adding multilingual support and working on technical SEO (structure, loading times and indexing) to strengthen lead generation from relevant searches.
vilaLancisV2.result: A clear, fast website ready to grow, presenting all advisory services in an organised way and helping businesses and freelancers understand what the firm offers and how to contact them without friction.

decos.problem: The handcrafted sandals online store was limited by an old platform. Managing products, updating content or improving the shopping experience required too much technical effort and negatively affected brand perception and sales.
decos.proposal: Migrate the store from Prestashop to WordPress with WooCommerce, keeping the catalogue while optimising performance, navigation and product pages. Define a clearer structure by collections, improve checkout and add a newsletter to retain customers.
decos.result: A more agile, easier-to-manage online store with a better shopping experience and a solid technical base to keep working on SEO, campaigns and seasonal launches without depending on third parties.

reset7.problem: The personal trainer didn’t have a landing page designed to turn visits into bookings. The value proposition wasn’t clear and there was no defined path for users to understand the service and take the next step.
reset7.proposal: Create a WordPress landing page focused on the RESET7 programme, with a clear message, sections explaining benefits and methodology, social proof and visible calls to action to request information or start the programme.
reset7.result: A results-oriented landing page that better communicates the value of the service, strengthens positioning for personal training searches and helps turn more visits into interested leads.

luciaNails.problem: The nail studio had a very basic online presence that didn’t reflect the style of the business or properly showcase services and past work. Potential clients struggled to get a clear idea of the offer before writing or booking.
luciaNails.proposal: Design and customise a WordPress site that puts the studio’s visual identity first, with clear sections for services, pricing and featured work, while taking care of performance and local SEO so new clients can discover the business.
luciaNails.result: A website aligned with the brand that builds trust at first glance, improves local visibility and helps more people arrive at the studio already understanding the services and style it offers.

fenix.problem: The mentoring programme for entrepreneurial mothers needed a website that conveyed authority, explained the value proposition and captured leads organically. Strong SEO was essential to drive sales without relying only on paid traffic.
fenix.proposal: Build the site in WordPress with Elementor, with a conversion-oriented structure (clear hero, programme levels, CTAs), SEO work for relevant searches and integration with Systeme.io for forms, sequences and lead capture.
fenix.result: A site that clearly communicates Fénix's offer (Despertar, Construcción, Transformación), improves visibility in search and channels visitors into the free programme and mentoring through Systeme.io.

cta.contact: I want to migrate my website without losing anything

when.intro: This service is for you if you find yourself in any of these situations:

agencyDependency.title: Agency dependency
agencyDependency.text: You don't have full control of your website or depend on third parties for any changes.

hardToMaintain.title: Hard to maintain website
hardToMaintain.text: The website works, but no one wants to touch it for fear of breaking something.

performanceIssues.title: Performance and technical issues
performanceIssues.text: The website is slow, gives errors or is not well updated.

needChange.title: Need for a clear change
needChange.text: You want a simpler, more stable and understandable solution.

when.extraText:
If right now you don't have clear access, depend on tickets or every change costs money,
migration is usually faster than you imagine.

what.intro: A migration is not just moving files. It's understanding what's there, what's unnecessary and how to leave the website ready for the future.

checklist.audit: Initial technical audit of current state
checklist.review: Review of access, hosting and structure
checklist.base: Clear technical base for future maintenance

analysis.title: Initial analysis
analysis.description: I review the current state of the website, access and risks before touching anything.

proposal.title: Migration proposal
proposal.description: I define a clear strategy explaining what is maintained and what is improved.

migration.title: Migration and validation
migration.description: I perform the migration in phases and validate that everything works correctly.

delivery.title: Delivery and documentation
delivery.description: I leave the website ready and documented so you don't have to depend on third parties again.

benefits.intro: The goal is not just to migrate, but to leave a better website than before.

maintenance.title: Easy maintenance
maintenance.text: A base designed so you can continue working without blocks.

projects.context: Some projects I’ve worked on include migrations, refactors and technical improvements on WordPress.

decos.problem: The handcrafted sandals store was tied to an agency and an outdated platform. Every change required tickets, response times were slow and the business had no clear view of what had been done technically or how it affected SEO and sales.
decos.proposal: Plan a controlled migration to WordPress with WooCommerce, recovering all key content and leaving behind unnecessary dependencies. I simplified the architecture, normalised access to hosting and admin, and left a store setup the business can understand and maintain.
decos.result: A more stable, faster online store with clear access and no agency dependency for every change. The business regained control over its catalogue and can keep working on SEO, campaigns and seasonal launches on top of a clean technical base.

jlgki.problem: The previous site depended on third parties for updates and changes, with a poorly documented stack that made everyone afraid to touch anything. Improvements were blocked by high costs and a lack of transparency about what the agency was actually doing.
jlgki.proposal: Rebuild the website in WordPress with a clear, documented structure, migrating content safely and reducing technical complexity. I defined access, hosting and processes so that future improvements don’t depend on a single agency again.
jlgki.result: A WordPress site optimised for speed and SEO, with a transparent, maintainable technical base. The team can now plan changes and new pages knowing what’s behind them and how much they’ll cost, without surprises or vendor lock-in.

vilaLancisV2.problem: The advisory firm came from an agency-managed website with long response times, hard-to-get access and poor performance. Small content or language adjustments kept turning into expensive, slow mini-projects.
vilaLancisV2.proposal: Migrate and redesign the site with WordPress, taking the project out of the agency circuit and putting access and documentation in the client’s hands. Content was restructured, multilingual support improved and performance optimised without losing rankings.
vilaLancisV2.result: A faster, clearer website with full control on the client side. They can now coordinate content, language and service changes without depending on third parties, while maintaining and improving SEO on a well-structured technical base.

hero.title: WordPress Emergency Rescue in 24h
hero.subtitle: Is your WordPress down, hacked, or showing critical errors? I'll help you recover it in less than 24 hours with guaranteed results.
hero.extraText: Immediate response. No intermediaries. Professional solution.

symptoms.title: Does your WordPress have any of these problems?

symptoms.intro: If your WordPress shows any of these symptoms, you need urgent help:

siteDown.title: Website down or inaccessible
siteDown.description: White screen, error 500, or simply won't load.

hacked.title: Hacked or infected
hacked.description: Strange content, unwanted redirects, Google security warning.

criticalError.title: WordPress critical error
criticalError.description: PHP error messages, database not accessible, plugins causing issues.

cannotAccess.title: Cannot access admin
cannotAccess.description: Locked out of admin panel, credentials don't work.

slowOrBroken.title: Extremely slow or broken
slowOrBroken.description: Infinite loading, unresponsive pages, broken functionality.

process.intro: My WordPress rescue process in 4 steps:

analysis.title: 1. Urgent Analysis (30 min)
analysis.description: Complete problem diagnosis: review logs, database, compromised files, and root causes.

backup.title: 2. Backup & Security (1h)
backup.description: Create complete backup before touching anything. If hacked, isolate the site to prevent further damage.

repair.title: 3. Repair (2-6h)
repair.description: Remove malware, repair database, restore files, update plugins, and fix the specific error.

prevention.title: 4. Prevention (1h)
prevention.description: Implement security measures, update everything, and give you clear recommendations to prevent recurrence.

services.intro: WordPress rescue services based on your situation:

emergency.title: Emergency Rescue
emergency.description:
Website down, hacked, or critical error.

Response in 2h. Solution in 24h.

malware.title: Malware Cleanup
malware.description:
Complete removal of viruses, malware, and malicious files.

Includes security hardening.

recovery.title: Backup Recovery
recovery.description:
Restoration from backup with minimal data loss.

Migration if needed.

maintenance.title: Preventive Maintenance
maintenance.description:
Updates, automatic backups, and monitoring.

Prevent future issues.

items.response: Response in less than 2 hours (business hours)
items.solution: Solution within 24 hours or full refund

howLong.question: How long does rescue take?
howLong.answer: Most rescues are resolved in 4-8 hours. In extreme cases it may take up to 24h. I'll keep you informed at every step.

guaranteed.question: Is it guaranteed?
guaranteed.answer: Yes. If I can't solve the problem, I'll refund 100% of the payment. In 5 years as a freelancer, I've never had to do it.

dataLoss.question: Will I lose data?
dataLoss.answer: No. I create a complete backup before touching anything. In the worst case, I can restore to the previous state.

afterHours.question: Do you work on weekends?
afterHours.answer: Yes. For urgent rescues I work any day. There's a small €30 surcharge for weekends.

cannotAccess.question: What if I don't have hosting access?
cannotAccess.answer: No problem. I can work with FTP, cPanel access, or even help you recover access if you've lost it.

prevention.question: How do I prevent it from happening again?
prevention.answer: After the rescue, I implement security measures and give you clear recommendations. Optionally, I offer monthly preventive maintenance.

cta.title: Does your WordPress need urgent rescue?
cta.subtitle: Contact now. Response in less than 2 hours.
